reviews.review_description: Any chance of seeing Quest Achievements in an update?
But the game itself is short with very little chalenge.  Cute game and a good deed.

The scenes, characters, music, environmental sounds, voice acting, story, are all very good, especially in the first half of this short game. There's a scene that invokes the feeling of Jurassic Park. Climbing is very fun. The puzzles are often too confusing (e.g. how do I feed this fruit to the animal?), which can be frustrating, though the beautiful scenery makes it more bearable. A few short cutscenes can be dizzy, otherwise it's very comfortable.
More of an experience with an emphasis on a fantasy setting, than a game. A bit dated now but it is fine.

I’m new to VR and saw this game. It’s short 1-2 hours and super cute. Helped me to learn the basics of VR movement. Loved the story and the graphics. Also taught me ways to overcome the nausea I was feeling using vr. Basically, had to keep my calves touching the couch so I had continuous bearings the real world, and then had to move my feet in place gently to give the brain the idea that the body was moving with what I was seeing in vr. Sitting made the nausea worse as I think the brain was confused by the conflicting message of interpreting movement but the body was not moving. 

I’ll play any games these guys make. Great for family!!!

This was also my first game in VR, so did help with learning it, the scenery was awesome did wish it was a little long to enjoy it more, was astonished with very huge then life Robert at the end through did like why wish it actually had put more then 1 dollar towards the fund would even have paid 5 more just to see at least 5 go towards the fund.  Thank you all for producing this marvel.

This game was extremely hard to follow and their were no clues or direction. I had no clue what to do. I got irritated and uninstalled it. My 15 yr old couldn’t even figure it out. It was really dark and hard to make anything out. Didn’t like it at all.

Despite appearing to have been made with good intentions and high production values, Rhythm of the Universe: IONIA is a strong example of so much that a VR game shouldn’t be.

Its graphics and sound design are very polished, and the world-building, though muddled, has strengths. But interactions from the player feel pointless, a segue between story elements that the developers are interested in, rather than the true core of a game.

The whole experience is burdened by an apparent need to deliver as much backstory and lore as possible, and, as a result, it’s often disjointed and confusing. Worse still, despite so much time given to this aspect, come the denouement we’re introduced to some pretty fundamental new concepts that, until that moment, have somehow gone entirely unmentioned.

For a full review, search YouTube for '6DOF Reviews Ionia'!

Im writing this review about ionia not to criticise
it, but to give more advise on its game play and
overall quality. In my opinion the games quite
lacking in length, and though the experience its
well thought of, it just doesn't last long enough.
I understood when i purchased the game that
it would be a story game, with puzzles and
some mystery involved, though i feel the game
could use with being more immersing, perhaps
puzzles requiring you to move around a 'larger'
and 'mysterious' map.
That being said, i absolutly love most of the
game play and encourage you to continue your
work. One more thing i would suggest would
be better graphic quality, i understand a lot of
players use Oculus quest 1, though stretching
the oculus quest 2's capabilities by adding
further aninations, sounds better graphics,
would im sure be appreciated by a lot of
players.
One more thing, would be trying to evolve the
game slightly, adding some more open world
gameplay, by making so the character can
more around into a lot more places with more
immersing objects, like plants that move when
you touch them e.t.c
A brilliant game cant want to experience future
updates!

developer_response.body:
Hi polvolter, thank you for taking the time to write a review. We are glad you enjoyed the game and we hear your request for more gameplay. Based on community feedback including yours we are actively working on expanding the gameplay to include more puzzle time and explorative space. You will see the next update in a week!
Kindly, Anna

reviews.review_description: Beautiful graphics and I love the subtext about saving the environment

Really, very short. 

Pros:
Lovely graphics 

Simple linear play

Puzzles (though Simple, so far)

Great for vr beginners 

Cons:
Grabbing and holding is a bit glitchy 


Leave your boundary and your immediately back to the start screen, having to begin at the beginning of whatever chapter you were in the middle of 

Trying to move during tutorial videos causes the game to lock up

reviews.review_title: I'm glad I took a chance on this.
reviews.review_description:
I'm glad I tried this, in spite of the negative reviews. Everybody likes something different. I loved this, the music, the characters, the world design, all very good.
I had no issues with the controls, or the puzzles. I appreciate that you can reach out and interact with various objects and plants.  
If I had a gripe, it was seeing the word 'Loading' between scenes. It took me out of the experience.  Better just a black screen for the moment. 
Overall, I really liked this, and I hope it succeeds so the devs can make more adventures.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words, jxo1968.

reviews.review_description: The promo video was kinda misleading and I feel like I wasted my time and money playing it. The sliding locomotion I typically use in other games was very slow in this one and I had to use the teleport function in order to get to different elevations in the game. Seemed cool at first but overall not worth the time, storage, or money

Glitching from the start. Literally. Took me forever to get the game to even register that I was trying to click “Play”. Didn’t get any better really. Couldn’t really turn while I was climbing unless standing. So this game cannot be played while sitting like it says. I could go on. I just don’t want to waste anymore time with this game.
Love love this game I can't believe it ended I was looking forward to a long adventure beautiful graphic design please please make another one
I saw the creators of this game at a Boston VR. It is the first VR game I ever tried!

reviews.review_title: The giant turtle is my favorite
reviews.review_description: enjoyable
Easy to play and no motion sickness. Very pretty!
One of the few games I don't have to kill anything.

reviews.review_title: Fun interactive experience - not a game
reviews.review_description:
This was a light and meditative experience. Similar to a park ride at Disney. It is geared for children but adults will get a kick out of the puzzles. The main reason I bought the game is because all proceeds will benefit the environmental non-profit Wildlife Warriors Worldwide, founded in 2002 by Steve and Terri Irwin. 
I gave four stars because I think the game can still have a few bug fixes including a save-along mode. I had to stop playing a few time and went back to the beginning of the chapter.
